name: Ascent to La Peñota via La Esfinge — circular route through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park
name_original: Subida a la Peñota por la Esfinge — circular por el Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Jogging around El Espinar offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama, characterized by mountain scenery and extensive forest areas. The region provides varied terrains, from challenging mountain trails to more accessible paths through natural enclaves. Runners can experience a mix of unpaved, gravel, and asphalt surfaces, making it suitable for different preferences. Best jogging routes around El Espinar The most popular jogging route is Ascent to La Peñota via La Esfinge — circular route through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views within the national park. Another top favourite among local runners is Refugio de la Salamanca – Cabeza Líjar Summit loop from Puerto de Guadarrama o Alto del León, a difficult 12.0 miles (19.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to a summit with expansive vistas. Local runners also love the La Peñota – La Peñota Peak loop from Puerto de Guadarrama o Alto del León, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Jogging around El Espinar is defined by mountain scenery, lush forests, and varied terrains, including historical paths like the Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría. The network offers options for all ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore El Espinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around El Espinar?
A: There are over 15 running routes around El Espinar, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy paths to very challenging mountain trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in El Espinar?
A: The running trails in El Espinar offer a diverse range of terrains. You can expect a mix of unpaved paths, gravel tracks, and some asphalt sections. Many routes traverse lush pine forests and mountainous areas within the Sierra de Guadarrama, providing varied surfaces and scenic views.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in El Espinar?
A: The running routes in El Espinar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 100 runners have already explored the varied terrain using komoot, often praising the breathtaking mountain scenery and the extensive network of trails.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier run?
A: Yes, El Espinar offers routes suitable for all abilities. While many are challenging, there are 1 easy route and 7 moderate routes that are great for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous run. These paths often provide a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there challenging running routes for experienced runners in El Espinar?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners, El Espinar boasts 10 difficult routes, many of which feature significant elevation gain and lead to summits with expansive vistas. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14891529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Fuenfría – La Peñota loop from Casa Forestal Molino del Caballo&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints in El Espinar?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its stunning viewpoints. Many routes, especially those in the Sierra de Guadarrama, offer panoramic vistas. You might encounter incredible landscapes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the views from summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/121253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seven Peaks&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maliciosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks I can explore while running in El Espinar?
A: Yes, you can combine your run with a journey through history. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, also known as Calzada borbónica, is a unique historical path that served as a primary route for centuries. It offers a distinct running experience through a significant piece of heritage.

Q: What natural features define the running experience in El Espinar?
A: The running experience in El Espinar is largely defined by its location within the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll encounter majestic peaks, lush pine forests, and diverse biodiversity. The region also features rivers running through natural areas, providing varied landscapes and fresh air for your run.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in El Espinar?
A: Yes, many of the routes around El Espinar are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164587462/ascent-to-la-penota-via-la-esfinge-circular-route-through-the-sierra-de-guadarrama-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to La Peñota via La Esfinge&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by mountain huts or shelters?
A: Yes, some routes in the area pass by mountain huts or shelters, which can be useful for a break or in case of changing weather. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14902945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Salamanca – Cabeza Líjar Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2636724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;. Other shelters like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pradera de Navalhorno Stone Shelter&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Valiente Shelter&lt;/a&gt; are also in the vicinity.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in El Espinar?
A: El Espinar offers several long-distance running trails for those looking to cover more ground. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14891529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Fuenfría – La Peñota loop&lt;/a&gt; extend over 26 kilometers, providing a significant challenge and ample opportunity for endurance training amidst beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there running routes accessible from San Rafael?
A: Yes, the nearby town of San Rafael serves as a convenient starting point for several running adventures. You can find routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19096235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from San Rafael&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 13-kilometer path through the local landscape.
tips: The signposted trail ends at the natural monument of the Peña del Arcipreste de Hita, a group of curious granite rocks where two large inscriptions appear. One of them is the stanza of the famous meeting between the poet and the shepherdess, and below it “Walker in this port one morning in March 1329”. The upper recording is a dedication: “1330/1930. "To the Archpriest of Hita, singer of this mountain range, who tasted the waters of the river of good love."
Almost like some sphinxes that are found around the world, we find this shape in the same stone before reaching the top of Peñota.
Within the Sierra de Guadarrama this is one of the highest mountains at the southern end of this mountain system. It reaches almost two thousand meters high!
